summary: The main claim is: The software efficiency overhang is the overhang influencing the most the takeoff speed.

In Does Davidad's Uploading Moonshot Work?, brings up the following: What about the idea of "just" training a giant transformer to, instead of predicting next tokens in natural language, predicting neural activity?
The main claim is: This post is a copy of the introduction of this paper on lie detection in LLMs.
Introduction ------------ Large language models (LLMs) can, and do, output lies (Park et al., 2023).
The main claim is: Therefore, those aiming to reduce AI existential risk should focus on AI Regulation, rather than on AI Alignment.
This is the evolutionary nature of the AI existential risk problem.
The main claim is: The people with real power would be his inferiors who have been there for a while and are part of the in-group.
The upper echelons of intelligence agencies are difficult to observe.
The main claim is: There will likely be enough resources to run many copies of a model once it has been trained.
How will models’ words per minute change in the future?
The main claim is: I don’t expect there are all that many more years with this much progress before we hit AGI — maybe 3-12 years.

I hold some hope that major warning shots and/or regulation would change this picture, in fact I think it’s pretty likely we’ll see warning shots beyond those we have already seen, but I am not too optimistic about what the response will b…
The main claim is: Of course, model parameter efficiency may increase in the future (as it happened with Chinchilla) - we should monitor this and potentially lower the cap.
The main claim is: Indeed, it's rare for people to say "haven't seen it" unless it's a two-way conversation.
The people who haven't seen it don't say anything, and so most of the answers come from people who have seen it, and have opinions on it.

top_comment.author: Eric Drexler
top_comment.text:
This makes sense, but LLMs can be described in multiple ways. From one perspective, as you say,

[...]

From different perspective (as you very nearly say) ChatGPT is simulating a skewed sample of people-and-situations in which the people actually do have answers to the question.

The contents of hallucinations are hard to understand as anything but token prediction, and by a system that (seemingly?) has no introspective knowledge of its knowledge. The model’s degree of confidence in a next-token prediction would be a poor indicator of degree of factuality: The choice of token might be uncertain, not because the answer itself is uncertain, but because there are many ways to express the same, correct, high-confidence answer. (ChatGPT agrees with this, and seems confident.)
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: This makes sense, but LLMs can be described in multiple ways.

summary: The main claim is: The aim is to allow existing ML researchers to quickly gauge interest in the subject based on their existing subfield skills and interests!
These documents list a subset of the various organizations and researchers involved in the AI safety space, along with major papers.

The main claim is: --- (Nitpick: I do want to push back a bit on their claim that they've "mechanistically understand the inner workings of optimized Transformers that learn in-context", since they've only really looked at the mechanism of how single layer a…
The paper argues that auto-regressive transformers implement in-context learning via gradient-based optimization on in-context data.
The main claim is: There are people not motivated to solve AI alignment, who do work related to AI alignment.
+ In general, if there are multiple ways to solve the problem, the solution we end up with will likely be more relevant for alignment.
The main claim is: Therefore, unless the existence odds take these facts into account - and I don't see evidence that they do - I think we need to revise the odds to be lower.
I believe that, if convergent instrumental subgoals don't imply alignment, that the original odds given are probably too low .
The main claim is: They're trying to build a god, and they're trying to do it right.
This is an obviously dangerous thing to avoid if you can avoid it.
